Sayed Salah
Database Administrator

sayedsalah.com
Education
SGBAU Amravati University CGPA: 7.21
Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) in Computer Science and Engineering 2014, Amravati, India
Experience
Database Administrator Full-time - On-Site
BNY Mellon Jan 2017 - Present, Cairo, Egypt
•Managed critical production, pre-production, test, and reporting databases across various platforms, overseeing
capacity management and upgrades.
•Handled day-to-day database activities, including proactive monitoring and incident management, adhering to ITIL
processes.
•Built and provisioned new databases as per requirements, preparing them for go-live in collaboration with multiple
teams.
•Executed scheduled database patching (CPU, PSU) and installed latest patches on production, development, and test
environments following Oracle recommendations.
•Successfully upgraded databases from Oracle 11g to 12c, ensuring seamless transition and minimal downtime.
•Expanded storage capacity by adding disks to ASM disk groups to support growing data volumes.
•Built and managed Disaster Recovery (DR) databases using Active Data Guard, ensuring synchronization with pro-
duction and resolving replication issues.
•Monitored and managed Data Guard configurations, including checking lagging status, resolving archive log gaps,
and monitoring RFS/MRP processes.
•Resolved tablespace-related issues, optimizing storage utilization and preventing outages.
•Managed user access and profiles, implementing robust security policies and ensuring compliance.
•Performed database imports and exports using Data Pump for data migration and replication tasks.
•Maintained a centralized inventory of all managed databases for streamlined tracking and management.
•Refreshed test environments from production databases to provide accurate and up-to-date data for development
and testing.
•Collaborated with Oracle Support to troubleshoot and resolve complex Oracle errors, ensuring timely resolution.
•Scheduled and managed daily and weekly database backups using RMAN, including troubleshooting RMAN-related
issues.
•Performed database cloning using RMAN for various purposes, including test environment provisioning.
•Participated in cutover activities to upgrade applications to higher versions, coordinating with application teams.
•Strictly followed ITIL processes in incident and change management, ensuring structured and controlled operations.
•Provided weekly reports on database issues in team meetings and actively participated in suggesting service im-
provement plans.
•Executed database migrations from one server to another or across different platforms, ensuring data integrity.
•Conducted Root Cause A

nalysis (RCA) and impact analysis reporting during production outages to prevent recur-
rence.
Database & System Administrator Full-time - On-Site
Brose India 2014 - Dec 2016, Pune, India
•Managed internal databases and servers across Brose’s global operations, ensuring stability and performance.
•Provided 24x7 on-call support in rotational shifts, responding to critical alerts and ad-hoc business user requests.
•Performed day-to-day database activities, monitoring DBA group emails for alerts and issues.
•Executed database creation and patching activities, maintaining up-to-date and secure environments.
•Managed frequent database backups using Data Pump and RMAN, ensuring data recoverability.
•Performed database refreshes using RMAN and Data Pump for development and testing purposes.

•Executed database recovery using data copies and RMAN, minimizing data loss during incidents.
•Monitored logs and traces for issue resolution, proactively identifying and addressing potential problems.
•Created new VM servers and prepared them for go-live, also managing decommissioning as per requirements.
•Conducted weekly patching of Windows servers using SCCM, ensuring system security and compliance.
•Monitored and troubleshooted daily and weekly OS backups using Symantec Netbackup.
•Managed user accounts for both OS and database users, maintaining access controls and security policies.
•Monitored OS-level alerts using SCOM, ensuring proactive response to system health issues.
•Project: Data Center Migration (Onsite Project)
•Successfully led and executed a data center migration project, relocating all servers and databases to a new facility.
•Installed Oracle 11g on Linux platforms, configuring for optimal performance and stability.
•Worked on service requests (Incidents / Change / Request), managing ticketing and resolution processes.
•Created users and managed user privileges, implementing granular access control for databases.
•Configured RMAN backup strategies for databases, ensuring robust disaster recovery capabilities.
•Performed patching of databases to maintain security and incorporate new features.
•Configured physical standby databases using Data Guard for high availability and disaster recovery.
•Cloned servers and migrated them to new clusters, optimizing infrastructure and resource utilization.
Skills
Databases: Oracle RDBMS (10g, 11g, 12c)
Technologies/Utilities: Data Pump - RMAN - Data Guard - ASM - RAC - Golden Gate - Netbackup - SCOM
- SCCM - VMWare vSphere
Tools: OCC - PUTTY - SQLPLUS - SQL Developer
Operating Systems: RHEL 6.0 - RHEL 6.5 - UNIX - Microsoft Windows

Sayed Salah is a Database Administrator with contact information including email (sayed.salah@email.com), phone number (+20 101 234 5678), GitHub profile (github.com/sayed-salah), LinkedIn profile (linkedin.com/in/sayed-salah), and personal website (sayedsalah.com).
Sayed Salah received a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science and Engineering from SGBAU Amravati University in 2014, achieving a CGPA of 7.21.

From December 2014 to December 2016, the Database & System Administrator managed internal databases and servers for Brose India in Pune, India, ensuring stability and performance, and provided 24x7 on-call support.

The administrator performed daily database activities, including monitoring alerts, executing creation and patching, managing backups with Data Pump and RMAN, and performing refreshes for development and testing.

The administrator executed database recovery using data copies and RMAN, monitored logs and traces for issue resolution, and managed VM servers, including creation, preparation for go-live, and decommissioning.

Weekly patching of Windows servers was conducted using SCCM, and daily/weekly OS backups were monitored and troubleshooted using Symantec Netbackup. User accounts for OS and database users were managed, maintaining access controls and security policies.
The administrator monitored OS-level alerts using SCOM and led a data center migration project, relocating servers and databases to a new facility. Oracle 11g was installed on Linux platforms.

The administrator worked on service requests (Incidents, Change, Request), managed ticketing and resolution, created users and managed privileges, configured RMAN backup strategies, performed database patching, and configured physical standby databases using Data Guard.

Servers were cloned and migrated to new clusters, optimizing infrastructure and resource utilization. The administrator possessed skills in Oracle RDBMS (10g, 11g, 12c), various technologies like Data Pump, RMAN, and Data Guard, and tools like OCC, PUTTY, and SQL Developer.

From January 2017 to the present, Sayed Salah has worked as a Database Administrator at BNY Mellon in Cairo, Egypt, managing databases across various platforms, handling incident management, building new databases, and performing patching and upgrades.
Sayed Salah successfully upgraded databases from Oracle 11g to 12c, expanded storage capacity using ASM disk groups, and managed Disaster Recovery databases with Active Data Guard.
Sayed Salah monitored Data Guard configurations, resolved tablespace issues, managed user access and security, performed data migration using Data Pump, and maintained a database inventory.

Sayed Salah refreshed test environments, collaborated with Oracle Support, scheduled database backups using RMAN, performed database cloning with RMAN, participated in application upgrades, followed ITIL processes, and provided weekly reports.
Sayed Salah executed database migrations, conducted Root Cause Analysis, and actively participated in service improvement plans.

India is a land of ancient rhythms and burgeoning modernity, a vast subcontinent cradling a civilization millennia old. Within its borders, cities pulse with life – Pune, a prominent hub nestled within its heart, a center for industry and technological advancement. It’s a place where skilled professionals, like the Database & System Administrator who, between 2014 and 2016, diligently maintained the digital backbone of companies like Brose India, ensuring seamless operations with round-the-clock dedication. This administrator, a product of India’s growing educational system – having earned a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science from SGBAU Amravati University in 2014 with a commendable CGPA of 7.21 – represents a new generation shaping the nation’s future. India is a crucible of talent, fostering expertise that fuels both local growth and global innovation. It’s a nation defined by its geographical scale, its historical depth, and the ambition of its people.

Data Pump is a powerful and versatile utility, a cornerstone in the daily life of a seasoned database administrator. Not a person, but a critical tool, it’s the workhorse behind efficient database backups, restorations, and – crucially – data migration. Think of it as a highly specialized mover, capable of swiftly and reliably transporting vast quantities of database information. 

It’s frequently employed alongside RMAN for comprehensive backup strategies, but truly shines when replicating or shifting data between databases, even across different versions of Oracle RDBMS (from 10g through 12c and beyond). Administrators like Sayed Salah rely on Data Pump to keep systems synchronized and refreshed, ensuring development and testing environments mirror production with accuracy. 

While not glamorous, Data Pump is essential for maintaining database health, enabling infrastructure optimization through server cloning and migration, and ultimately, keeping critical data flowing where it needs to be. It’s a silent partner in the administrator’s toolkit, a testament to the power of efficient data handling.

Sayed Salah is a meticulous and highly capable Database Administrator currently shaping the data landscape at BNY Mellon in Cairo, Egypt. Since 2017, he’s been a key figure in maintaining and evolving their database systems, a role he approaches with a blend of proactive management and swift problem-solving. A 2014 graduate of SGBAU Amravati University with a Bachelor’s in Computer Science and Engineering (achieving a commendable 7.21 CGPA), Sayed isn’t simply a maintainer – he’s a builder and an improver. 

He’s demonstrated expertise in critical upgrades, notably leading the migration from Oracle 11g to 12c, and skillfully expanding storage capacity through ASM disk groups. Disaster Recovery is a particular strength, evidenced by his management of Active Data Guard configurations and diligent monitoring of Data Guard performance. He’s equally comfortable with the granular details, routinely resolving tablespace issues and meticulously managing user access and security. 

Sayed’s toolkit is comprehensive, encompassing Data Pump for seamless data migration, RMAN for robust backups and cloning, and a collaborative spirit that extends to Oracle Support. He’s a firm believer in structured processes, adhering to ITIL frameworks and providing clear, weekly reports on database health. Beyond routine maintenance, he actively participates in service improvement plans, driving root cause analysis to prevent future issues and ensuring the continued reliability of critical systems. He maintains a professional presence online, accessible via his LinkedIn profile (linkedin.com/in/sayed-salah), GitHub (github.com/sayed-salah), and personal website (sayedsalah.com), and can be reached at sayed.salah@email.com or +20 101 234 5678.

Data Guard is a stalwart guardian of critical database systems, a robust framework for disaster recovery and high availability. It’s not a flashy tool, but a quietly powerful one, meticulously configured and monitored to ensure business continuity. Think of it as a shadow database, a perfect replica constantly synchronized with its primary, ready to step into the spotlight should the original falter. 

Administrators, like Sayed Salah, are its dedicated custodians, shaping its defenses through careful configuration of RMAN backup strategies and the intricate setup of physical standby databases. They watch over Data Guard’s health, swiftly resolving tablespace issues and ensuring the replication process remains unbroken. It demands a deep understanding of Oracle RDBMS – from the seasoned 10g to the modern 12c – and mastery of tools like Data Pump for seamless data migration. 

Data Guard isn’t a standalone entity; it thrives within a broader ecosystem. It’s intimately linked to the administrator’s skillset, a testament to their expertise in safeguarding data. It’s a key component in larger infrastructure projects, enabling server cloning and migration to optimized clusters. While often unseen, Data Guard is the silent partner in maintaining a resilient and secure database environment, a critical line of defense against data loss and downtime.

The heart of Brose India’s operations in Pune, these databases weren’t simply repositories of data, but vital organs demanding constant vigilance. From December 2014 to December 2016, a dedicated administrator served as their tireless guardian, ensuring 24/7 stability and performance. Daily life revolved around a meticulous rhythm: monitoring alerts, orchestrating creation and patching, and safeguarding against loss with robust backups utilizing both Data Pump and RMAN. These weren’t static entities; they were constantly refreshed, providing fertile ground for development and testing. 

Beyond the daily routines, a weekly cadence of Windows server patching via SCCM and diligent monitoring of OS backups through Symantec Netbackup kept the system resilient. Access was carefully controlled, with user accounts – both for the operating system and the databases themselves – meticulously managed to uphold strict security policies. The administrator wasn’t merely reactive, however. Proactive monitoring through SCOM caught potential issues before they blossomed, and a significant undertaking – a complete data center migration – saw these databases physically relocated to a new facility, a testament to their importance. 

Oracle 11g, expertly installed on Linux platforms, formed a core component, further bolstered by the configuration of physical standby databases using Data Guard, a safeguard against unforeseen disruptions. Service requests – incidents, changes, and simple requests – flowed through the administrator’s hands, each ticket a challenge met with resolution. Ultimately, these databases weren’t isolated systems, but integral to Brose India’s functioning, carefully nurtured and protected by a dedicated expert.

RMAN – Recovery Manager – is a cornerstone of database resilience, a powerful utility wielded by skilled administrators to safeguard and restore Oracle databases. It isn’t a flashy tool, but a quiet guardian, diligently working behind the scenes to ensure data integrity and availability. RMAN isn’t simply about *making* backups; it’s about orchestrating a comprehensive recovery strategy. Administrators configure RMAN with carefully considered backup policies, defining what data is protected, how often, and where it’s stored – be it on disk or tape. 

When disaster strikes, or even a simple error occurs, RMAN steps forward. It’s the instrument used to execute precise database recovery, leveraging those pre-existing backups to rewind time and restore the database to a consistent state. Beyond basic recovery, RMAN excels at more nuanced tasks – cloning databases for development and testing, efficiently refreshing environments with up-to-date data, and even facilitating complex migrations. 

Individuals like Sayed Salah rely on RMAN daily, scheduling backups, performing clones, and ensuring the smooth operation of critical database systems. It’s a skill set deeply intertwined with broader database administration expertise, often paired with proficiency in tools like Data Pump and Data Guard. RMAN isn’t a standalone solution, but a vital component within a larger ecosystem of database management, a testament to its enduring importance in maintaining the health and longevity of Oracle databases.
